page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-heavy virtual machine instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, featuring 22 physical cores and 44 vCPUs via hyperthreading on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 19,326 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across six local drives, with no integrated GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in specific multi-threaded and cryptographic workloads. The instance achieves a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 231.48 GB/sec and a strong PassMark CPU encryption test score of 38,504.45. Image processing performance is also strong, registering a Geekbench score of 15,970.0. Most other standard compute, memory, and database benchmarks fall within the average range, including a PassMark database operations score of 18,226.03 and a Redis SET throughput of 50.68 million operations per second.

While the server demonstrates strong prompt processing for medium 7B LLM models (181.41 tokens/sec) and text generation for small 135M models (310.28 tokens/sec), it exhibits a significant performance bottleneck during large-scale AI tasks. Specifically, text generation using a large 70B LLM model drops to a poor 2.36 tokens/sec, indicating it is not suited for large-scale local LLM text generation without GPU acceleration. Given the high memory capacity and massive local NVMe SSD storage, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and high-throughput storage workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-heavy virtual machine featuring 44 vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz. It integrates 19,326 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives, with no GPU hardware. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (231.48 GB/sec), CPU encryption, and image processing, while standard database and memory bandwidth tests perform in the average tier. A notable tradeoff is its poor text generation speed of 2.36 tokens/sec on large 70B LLM models, highlighting its limitations for heavy AI inference. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput storage applications, and data-processing workloads that benefit from high resource density and massive local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized instance with 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and 19.3 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and data-processing workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd features 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and 19.3 TB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 44 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors
19,326 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across six drives
Strong performance in multi-threaded decompression and CPU encryption
Poor text generation performance on large 70B LLM models
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-throughput data processing
